Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The process starts with Receive order which is the first step After that the next step is Check inventory

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The description says that the inventory check must occur before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment So Check inventory is a prerequisite for both payment authorization and fulfillment steps like packaging shipping etc

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now if the inventory is sufficient the process should go Receive Check Authorize Payment Package items Ship order Close order Thats the full sequence for when inventory is enough

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: If the inventory isnt sufficient the process should branch to Cancel order after the check then proceed to Close order So the sequence here is Receive Check Cancel Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The description also mentions that after cancellation its illogical to do fulfillment steps like packaging or shipping So those shouldnt happen after a cancellation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive Check Authorize Payment Package Ship Close This follows the full sequence when inventory is sufficient All steps in order No violations here So Case 1 is compliant

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive Check Package Authorize Payment Ship Close Wait the order here is Check Package before Authorize Payment According to the normative process Authorize Payment should come before Package

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So in Case 2 Package comes before Authorize Payment Thats a violation This case violates the rule that Authorize Payment must occur before packaging

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive Check Authorize Payment Cancel Ship Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: But here they authorized payment before canceling So maybe the inventory was sufficient here but then they canceled

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But the problem is that if they canceled they should not have shipped Wait the process says that if inventory is insufficient after Check Cancel Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So after Cancel they shipped which is a violation The Ship after Cancel is invalid So this case violates the rule that shipping cant happen after a cancellation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive Check Cancel Close This is exactly the sequence for when inventory is insufficient So no violations here

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive Check Authorize Payment Ship Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The steps here are Check Authorize Payment Ship but missing Package items before shipping According to the normal flow after Authorize Payment comes Package items then Ship

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So Case 5 violates the rule that shipping must follow packaging
